hadiths.chapter: 2. SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS
hadiths.ar:
62  -  وَّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّالَّ لِن َّعْض ِ ن ِسَّان ِهِ 
ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ مَّطْهَّزَّة   لِلْحَّوَّإس ِى  وَّ مَّذ ْهَّن َّه   لِلْن َّوَّإسِي زِ . 
ولأ ي ح ور  إلي ع وط ق ى  ق ي ى  إلن ز إل وي حث  إلأ س ح ار إلمي مزة ، وإلعله  ق ى  ذ لك :
hadiths.en:
It is narrated "Whoever speaks while in the restroom will not have his needs 
fulfilled”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 2, H. 61

hadiths.chapter: 2. SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS
hadiths.ar:
63   -  مَّا ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْن َّاق ِزُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «وي َّعَّالَّىمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ً وَّك َّلَّهُّمْ ن ِن َّن َّاب ِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ مِن َّ إَّلس  َّح َّزِ وَّ إَّلن  َّح ْلِ  
ق َّلَّي ْسَّ مِن ْ س َّح َّزَّة ٍ وَّ لأَّ ن َّح ْلَّه ٍ إ ِلأ َّ وَّ مَّعَّهَّا مِن َّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ مَّلَّك   ي َّحْق َّط ُّهَّا وَّ مَّا كَّان َّ مِن ْهَّا   وَّ لَّوْ لأَّ إ َّن  َّ مَّعَّهَّا مَّن ْ ي َّمْن َّعُّهَّا 
لَّأ َّكَّلَّي ْهَّ ا إَّلسِّن َّاعُّ وَّ هَّوَّإم ُّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ ق ِي هَّا ي َّمَّزَّي ُّهَّا .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, said to some of his wives:  
"Tell the believing women to perform istinja with water and to be thorough in it, for it is 
a means of purification for the private parts and helps prevent hemorrhoids."  
[AL SADUQ] 
It is not permissible to relieve oneself in shaded areas or under fruit-bearing trees. The 
reasoning for this is: (continues to next Hadith) 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 2, H. 62

hadiths.chapter: 2. SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS
hadiths.ar:
64   -  وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ن َّهَّى رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ َّن ْ ي َّص ْزِب َّ إ َّجَّذ  مِن َّ إَّلْمُّسْلِمِي ن َّ ج َّلاَّءَّة ي َّحْث َّ س َّح َّزَّة ٍ إ َّوْ ن َّح ْلَّه ٍ ق َّذْ  
إ َّي ْمَّزَّب ْ لِمَّكَّان ِ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ِ إَّلْمُّوَّك َّلِي ن َّ ن ِهَّا إ كَّان َّ ق ِي هِ جَّمْلُّهُّ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه َّ 
ي َّحْص ُّزُّةُّ .  
ومن  لأ ي ن ق طغ ن وله وي ع لن ه ق الله إ ولى ن العذ ر ق لي ي ق  علي ه ما إسي طاع ولي ي ح ذ  ج زي طه .  
ومن  ن ال ولم ي ي ع وط ق لي س علي ه إلأسي ن ح اء وإ ن ما علي ه ع سل ذ كزة، ومن  ي ع وط ولم ي ن ل ق لي س علي ه إ ن  ي ع سل ذ كزة وإ ن ما 
علي ه إ ن  ي سي ن ح ى .  
ومن  ي وص ا  ي م ج زج ث  من ه ري ح ق لي س علي ه إلأسي ن ح اء وإ ن ما علي ه إ عاذة  إلوص وء.
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, has appointed angels to protect the 
vegetation of the earth, including trees and palm trees. There is no tree or palm except 
that there is an angel with it from Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, who guards it. If it 
were not for the presence of those angels to protect them, wild animals and pests 
would consume their fruits." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 56, p. 239 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 2, H. 63

hadiths.chapter: 2. SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS
hadiths.ar:
65  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ: إ َّن  َّ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ كَّان َّ ي َّسْي َّي ْق ِظ ُّ مِن ْ ن َّوْمِهِ ق َّي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّسْي ن ْح ِى   وَّق َّالَّ  كَّالْمُّي َّعَّح ِّث ِ  
مِن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ سَّم َّاة .
hadiths.en:
(Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} ) has narrated: "The Messenger of Allah 
{saws} prohibited any Muslim from relieving himself under a tree or palm that has borne 
fruit due to the presence of the angels assigned to them. Imam {a.s} said:  
'That is why trees and palms seem luster when they bear fruit, for the angels are 
present there.'". 
[AL SADUQ] 
For one who suffers from a continual inability to control their urine, Allah {SWT} is more 
deserving of the excuse. They should take precautions as much as they can and 
should use a cloth (to protect themselves). (means to prevent urine from spreading).  
If someone urinates but does not defecate, they are not required to perform istinja; 
they only need to wash their private part. If someone defecates but does not urinate, 
they are not required to wash their private part, but they must perform istinja. 
If someone performs ablution and then passes gas, they are not required to perform 
istinja; they only need to repeat their ablution. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 327 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 2, H. 64

reference.location: Volume 1 › SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS

hadiths.chapter: 2. SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS
hadiths.en:
It was narrated that Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} would awaken 
from his sleep, perform ablution, and not perform istinja.  
He expressed astonishment at a man whom he referred to, saying, "I have heard that 
when he passes gas, he performs istinja." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 44 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 135 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 345

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 2, H. 65

reference.location: Volume 1 › SEEKING PLACE TO RELIEVE ONESELF AND MANNERS

hadiths.chapter: 3. SECTIONS OF SALAT
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The prayer consists of three parts: one-third is for purification, one-third is for bowing 
(ruku), and one-third is for prostration (sujud) " 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 273 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 
140 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 365 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 42 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 366 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 6, 
p. 310 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, vol. 6, p. 389 • A l-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 20 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p.

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 3, H. 66
reference.chapter: SECTIONS OF SALAT
reference.location: Volume 1 › SECTIONS OF SALAT

hadiths.chapter: 4. THE TIMES WHEN PURIFICATION IS AN OBLIGATION
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"When the time for prayer enters, purification (ablution) and the prayer become 
obligatory, and there is no prayer without purification." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 140 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 43 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 372 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 203

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 4, H. 67

hadiths.chapter: 5. THE INITIATION OF SALAT, ITS PROHIBITIONS AND
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, said:  
"The initiation of prayer is purification (ablution), its prohibition is the takbir (saying 
'Allahu Akbar'), and its permissibility is the Tasleem (saying 'Assalamu Alaikum')."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 69 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 365 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol. 7, p. 43 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 366 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 6, p. 11 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 6, p. 415 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 6, p. 417

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 5, H. 68

hadiths.chapter: 7. THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
hadiths.ar:
70  -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
ق َّا ُّولَّن ِك َّ عَّلَّى ج ِلاَّف ِ سُّن  َّي ِى  وَّ إَّلي  َّان ِث ُّ عَّلَّى سُّن  َّي ِى  مَّعِى  ق ِى  جَّط ِي زَّة ِ إَّلْق ُّذْسِ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
"For ghusl (ritual washing), a saa' of water is required, and for wudu (ablution), a mud 
of water is sufficient.  
The saa' of The Prophet {saws} is five amdad (measures), and the mud weighs two 
hundred and eighty dirhams. One dirham is six danaq (small units), and one danaq 
weighs six grains.  
The grain should be from medium-quality barley, not from the small or large types”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Ma’ani al-Akhbar, Part 1, p. 249 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 
1, p. 135 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 121 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 314 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 481 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 77, p. 350 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 7, H. 69
reference.chapter: THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧، ح ٦٩

hadiths.chapter: 7. THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
hadiths.ar:
71  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إِجْي َّاح َّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْوُّص ُّوءِ لِلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّق ْذِرْ عَّلَّى إَّلْمَّاءِ ق َّوَّج َّذَّ  
مَّاءً ن ِق َّذْرِ مَّا ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ ن ِهِ ن ِمِان َّه ِ ذِرْهَّمٍ هَّلْ ي َّح ِث ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ َّن ْ ي َّس ْي َّزِي َّهُّ وَّ ي َّي َّوَّص  َّا َّ ن ِهِ إ َّوْ ي َّي َّي َّ م َّمَّ ق َّق َّال صَّان َّن ِى   
مِي ْلُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّاس ْي َّزَّي ْث ُّ وَّ ي َّوَّص  َّا ْب ُّ وَّ مَّا ي َّسُّوو ُّن ِى  ن ِذ َّلِك َّ مَّال  كَّي ِي ز  .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Ablution (wudu) is a mud (a specific measure), and ghusl (ritual washing) is a saa' (a 
larger measure). There will come people after me who will underestimate these 
amounts, and those will be contrary to my Sunnah.  
The one who adheres to my Sunnah will be with me in the enclosure of holiness 
(Paradise)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Ash'athiyat, Part 1, p. 16 • Al-Wadar (Lil-Rawandi) Vol. 
1, p. 44 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 313 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 483 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Part 1, p. 347 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 7, H. 70
reference.chapter: THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧، ح ٧٠

hadiths.chapter: 7. THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
hadiths.ar:
72  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إ ِن َّاءٍ وَّإجِذٍ ق َّزْج َّهُّ ي ُّم َّ ص َّزَّ ن َّث ْ هِى َّ  
ق َّا َّن ْق َّث ْ ق َّزْج َّهَّا ي ُّم َّ إ َّق َّاض َّ هُّوَّ وَّ إ َّق َّاص َّث ْ هِى َّ عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِهَّا جَّي  َّى ق َّزَّع َّا وَّ كَّان َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  إِع ْي َّسَّلَّ ن ِهِ إَّ لن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ  
إ لِهِ ي َّلاَّي َّه َّ إ َّمْذَّإذٍ وَّ إَّل َّذ ِى  إِع ْي َّسَّلَّث ْ ن ِهِ مُّذ َّي ْن ِ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا إ َّج ْز َّ إ َّ عَّن ْهُّمَّا لِأ َّن  َّهُّمَّا إِس ْي َّزَّكَّا ق ِي هِ ج َّمِي عاً وَّ مَّن ِ إِن ْق َّزَّذَّ ن ِالْع ُّسْلِ وَّجْذَّةُّ 
ق َّلاَّ ن ُّذ َّ لَّهُّ مِن ْ صَّاعٍ .    
ولأن ذ للوص وء من  ي لاي ه  إ كف 
ق زق .
hadiths.en:
Abu Al -Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar -Ridha {a.s} was asked about a man who 
needed to perform wudu for prayer but could not find water, except for a quantity of 
water worth a hundred dirhams. Should he buy it to perform wudu or should he 
perform tayammum (dry ablution)? 
Imam {a.s} said: ""Rather, he should buy it. I have experienced a similar situation, and I 
bought it and performed wudu and I would not mind having that money”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 370 • Muskkin al -Fu’ād, 
Part 1, p. 141 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 523 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 243 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 356 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 7, H. 71
reference.chapter: THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧، ح ٧١

hadiths.chapter: 7. THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
hadiths.en:
Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} and his wife performed ghusl using five amdad from one 
container." 
Zurarah then asked how it was done. 
Imam {a.s} said: "He started by immersing his hand in the water before hers and 
cleansed his private part. Then She did the same and cleansed her private part. After 
that, he poured water over himself, and She poured water over herself until they were 
finished. The amount that the Prophet used for ghusl was three amdad, while She used 
two muds. This was sufficient for both of them because they shared the same water.  
However, for someone who performs ghusl alone, they must use a saa'."  
[AL SADUQ] 
For wudu, three handfuls of water are required: one for the face and two for the arms. 
If someone can only manage one handful of water, they should divide it into three 
parts. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 7, H. 72
reference.chapter: THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧، ح ٧٢

hadiths.chapter: 7. THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"A man may worship Allah {SWT} for forty years but does not truly obey Him in wudu 
(ablution) because he washes what Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, commanded him 
to wipe."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 295 • Tafsir al -Safi, Part 2, p. 17 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 422

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 7, H. 73
reference.chapter: THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE AMOUNT OF WATER FOR ABLUTION AND GHUSL
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧، ح ٧٣

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.ar:
75 - وَّ رُّوِى مُّع ِي زَّة ُّ إ َّ ن َّسِي ث َّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ 
إَّلل َّهِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Shall I not describe the ablution of the Messenger of Allah {saws} to you?"  
They replied: "Yes."  
Imam {a.s} then called for a bowl containing some water and placed it in front of him.  
He rolled up his sleeves, dipped his right hand into the water, and said: "This is if the 
hand is pure."  
Imam {a.s} then scooped a full handful of water, placed it on his forehead, "In the name 
of Allah {SWT}," allowing the water to flow over the edges of his beard. 
Then Imam {a.s} passed his hand over his face and the front of his temples once.  
Imam {a.s} dipped his left hand into the water, scooped a full handful, and placed it on 
his right elbow, running his hand along his forearm until the water flowed over the tips 
of his fingers.  
Imam {a.s} then scooped with his right hand again and placed it on his left elbow, 
running his hand along his forearm until the water flowed over the tips of his fingers.  
He then wiped front of his head and the tops of his feet with the leftover moisture from 
the water. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 74
reference.chapter: THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.ar:
76   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ مَّز َّة ً مَّز َّة ً  ن ِهِ .  
ق ا ما إلأج ن ار إلي ى  روي ث  ق ى  إ ن  إلوص وء مزي ي ن  مزي ي ن :
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} performed wudu and then wiped over his sandals.  
Al-Mughirah said to him: "Did you forget, O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}?"  
He {saws} replied: "No, rather it is you who has forgotten. This is how my Lord {AZJ} 
commanded me."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 286 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 460 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 75

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.ar:
77   -  ن ِا ِسْن َّاذٍ مُّن ْق َّطِغٍ ي َّزْوِي هِ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْأ َّجْوَّلُّ ذ َّكَّزَّةُّ عَّم َّن ْ رَّوَّإةُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
إَّلْوُّص ُّوءَّ وَّإجِذَّة ً وَّإجِذَّة ً وَّ وَّص َّغَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لِلن  َّاسِ إِي ْن َّي َّي ْن ِ إِي ْن َّي َّي ْ ن ِ .  
وهذ إ على ج هه  إلأن كار، لأ على ج هه  إلأج ن ار، كا ن ه علي ه إلسلام ي ق ول: جذ إلله جذإ ق ي ح اور ة رسول إلله صلى إلله علي ه وإ له 
وي عذإة وق ذ ق ال إلله ي عالى: " ۝ وَّمَّن  ي َّي َّعَّذ َّ جُّذُّوذَّ إ لل َّهِ ق َّق َّذْ ط َّلَّمَّ ن َّق ْسَّهُّ  ۝.
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"By Allah {SWT}, the ablution of the Messenger of Allah {saws} was performed only once for 
each part, and The Prophet {saws} performed wudu once for each action."  
Imam {a.s} said: "This is an ablution that Allah {SWT} does not accept the prayer without 
it”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
As for the reports that have been narrated about performing wudu twice for each 
action: (continues to next hadith) 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 318 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 438 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 76
reference.chapter: THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.ar:
78  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى إَّلْمُّو ْمِن َّ لأَّ ي ُّن َّح ِّسُّهُّ س َّى ْء  
وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّكْق ِي هِ مِي ْلُّ إَّلذ َّهْن ِ .
hadiths.en:
In a broken chain of narration, Abu Ja'far al -Ahwal recounts from those who 
narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} who said: "Allah {SWT} made ablution (wudu) obligatory 
once for each action, but the Messenger of Allah {saws} prescribed it twice for the 
people”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
The chain of narration is disconnected, and this is in the form of denial (that Prophet 
‘exceeded’ the limit of Allah {SWT}), and not informing (of the limit being exceeded).  
Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} recalled this report is in the context to refute it, and not to ‘report’ 
it.  
This is stated in a manner of condemnation, rather than mere reporting, as if He {a.s} is 
saying: ‘Allah {SWT} has set a limit, and the Messenger of Allah {saws} has ‘exceeded’ it? 
(How can this be?) when Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has said: "And whoever transgresses 
the limits of Allah {SWT} has certainly wronged himself."’ (Surah At Talaq 65:1) 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 318 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 439 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 77
reference.chapter: THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨، ح ٧٧

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated (by Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} ) that:  
"Ablution is a limit set by Allah {SWT} so that He may know who obeys Him and who 
disobeys Him. And a believer is not made impure by anything ; rather, something like 
oil is sufficient for him”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 21 • Ilal al -Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 279 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 138 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 309 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 438 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, 
vol. 1, p. 484 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 127 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 288 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 78

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.ar:
80   -  وَّ ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ جَّذِي ث   إ ج َّزُّ ن ِا ِسْن َّاذٍ مُّن ْق َّطِغٍ رَّوَّإةُّ عَّمْزُّو ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْمِق ْذَّإمِ ق َّالَّ جَّذ َّي َّن ِى  مَّن ْ سَّمِغَّ إ َّن ا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول   إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ 
إِي ْن َّي َّي ْن ِ إِي ْن َّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ كَّان َّ ي ُّ ح َّذِّذُّ إَّلْوُّص ُّوءَّ لِكُّلِّ ق َّزِي ص َّه ٍ وَّ لِكُّلِّ صَّلاَّة ٍ .  
ق معن ى إلحذي ث  هو إن ى  لأ عح ث  ممن  ي زع ث  عن  ي ح ذي ذ إلوص وء وق ذ ج ذذة إلن ن ى  صلى إلله علي ه وإ له، وإلح ن ز إلذ ى  روى  "  
إ ن  من  ر إذ على مزي ي ن  لم ي و ج ز " ي و كذ ما ذ كزي ه   ومعن اة إ ن  ي ح ذي ذة ن عذ إلي ح ذي ذ لأ إ ج ز له   كالأذ إن ، من  صلى إلط هز وإلعصز 
ن ا ذ إن  وإ ق امي ي ن  إ ج ز إ ة ومن  إ ذ ن  للعصز كان  إ ق ص ل، وإلأذ إن  إلي الث  ن ذعه  لأ إ ج ز له، وكذ لك  ما روى  إ ن  مزي ي ن  إ ق ص ل معن اة  
إلي ح ذي ذ، وكذ لك  ما روى  مزي ي ن  إ ن ه إ سن اع .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever exceeds the prescribed limits in his ablution (wudu) is like one who nullifies 
it”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 313 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 438 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 79
reference.chapter: THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨، ح ٧٩

hadiths.chapter: 8. THE DESCRIPTION OF THE ABLUTION OF THE
hadiths.en:
In another narration with a broken chain, Amr ibn Abi al -Miqdam said: "I was 
informed by someone who heard Abu Abdullah {a.s} say: 'I am amazed at those who 
prefer to perform wudu twice, while the Messenger of Allah {saws} performed it twice. 
The Prophet {saws} would renew his ablution for each obligatory prayer and each 
prayer.'" 
[AL SADUQ] 
The meaning of this narration is that I am astonished at those who neglect to renew 
their ablution when the Prophet did so. The report that states "Whoever exceeds twice 
will not be rewarded" supports what I have mentioned. It means that renewing wudu 
after it has already been done does not carry a reward, similar to the call to prayer 
(adhan). For instance, one who prays Dhuhr and Asr with one adhan and two iqamah 
suffices, and it is better to call the adhan for Asr as well. The third call to prayer is an 
innovation with no reward. The reports indicating that twice is better imply the renewal 
of wudu, and those mentioning two times indicate its thoroughness (i.e., performing it 
well). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 8, H. 80
